questions.question: What is the theoretical maximum simultaneous outbound flows a single public IP can sustain with ephemeral range 32768-60999?
questions.explanation: The 16-bit port space is bounded; the OS ephemeral range 32768-60999 gives 28232 ports, one per outbound 5-tuple under symmetric NAPT.

questions.question: Users see intermittent 'connect: Cannot assign requested address' only during peak hours. What is the first metric to check?
options: conntrack established count and insert_failed counter
questions.explanation: insert_failed climbing while established plateaus is the signature of NAT port exhaustion; the failure happens before packets leave the host.

questions.question: Hairpin NAT loopback most commonly breaks because:
options: The DNS TTL is too long
The inside server cannot demultiplex SYN-ACKs whose source was rewritten to the public IP
The switch does not support jumbo frames
questions.explanation: Hairpin rewrites both source and destination; if the return 5-tuple collides the server cannot match the SYN to an existing socket.
